preview_text:
The model favours Norway at 64% to win, with Iraq positioned as a clear underdog at 12%. The gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast; Norway is heavily favoured to take the match, though not at prohibitive odds.

The draw carries material weight in the model's assessment at 25%, suggesting a reasonably competitive encounter rather than a runaway outcome. The spread across all three outcomes indicates meaningful uncertainty around Norway's margin of victory, even as their win probability remains dominant. A draw emerges as the likeliest alternative to a Norway success.

The market consensus diverges from the model on both tail outcomes. Sportsbooks price Norway's win probability 13 percentage points higher at 76%, while assigning the draw only 15% rather than 25%. This systematic shift compresses Iraq's chances further, to 8%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these divergent views; both reflect coherent but distinct assessments of the match.
